task: answer 'Are you back where you started?' in natural language, from a sequence of moves
setup: The path reaches the model ONLY through the trajectory cortex (no coordinates in the text, which holds just the question). The cortex is pre-trained on path integration and frozen; the LLM then learns to read its spatial representation -> yes/no.
ablation_control: cortex OFF (zeroed) -> 51.3% = chance, so the LLM's answers come from the spatial cortex, not the question-only text.

supervised_pretrain.note: cortex pre-trained by regressing the ground-truth final (x,y,z) — a scaffold that uses position labels the brain never receives.

selfsup_pretrain.note: cortex pre-trained by predicting the place-cell code of the destination (a fixed sensory function of position); NO coordinate labels. CPU probe: 'back-at-start' is ~100% readable from the no-label representation, so the LLM should read it as well as the supervised version.

caveat: The cortex is pre-trained before the LLM, not learned end-to-end (end-to-end from one yes/no token collapses to the class prior). This is defensible: (1) the brain's grid/place/head-direction system is largely innate/developmentally scaffolded and present in pups before extensive experience (Langston et al. 2010; Wills et al. 2010); (2) the sensorimotor stage precedes language in development. The unrealistic part of the SUPERVISED run is the position LABELS; the SELF-SUPERVISED protocol removes them (predict a sensory function of position, recover position internally — Banino et al. 2018; Cueva & Wei 2018).
